# Patti Smith Real Estate, Georgetown, California

> Resident real estate broker serving Georgetown and the Georgetown Divide in El Dorado County, California. Specialist in rural and foothill property: acreage, wells and septic, fire hazard zones, equestrian and ranch property, and Gold Country homes.

## About
- Broker: Patti Smith (Patti Smith Real Estate, Independent)
- License: CA DRE #01110483
- Office: 6180 State Highway 193, Georgetown, CA 95634
- Phone: (530) 368-0852
- Serving the Sierra Nevada foothills since 1992
- Georgetown resident; past president of the Georgetown Rotary Club; 12-year director of the Georgetown Fire Department

## Georgetown, CA at a glance
- Founded August 7, 1849; California Historical Landmark No. 484; nicknamed Growlersburg, later the "Pride of the Mountains"
- Unincorporated census-designated place in El Dorado County; elevation about 2,600 feet; population roughly 2,255 (2020 census)
- Above the valley fog and below the snow line; warm-summer Mediterranean climate; about four times the valley's rainfall
- Western gateway to the Rubicon Trail; home of the Jeepers Jamboree (since 1953)
- Water: Georgetown Divide Public Utility District (Stumpy Meadows Reservoir); many rural parcels on private wells and septic
- Schools: Black Oak Mine Unified School District (headquartered in Georgetown)
- Typical market: move-ready homes on acreage roughly $400,000 to $700,000; entry cabins and fixers from the low $200,000s; raw land from about $50,000
